segments.title: Paddle battleship as science-fiction naval logistics
segments.summary: The stream uses Glenn Stewart's paddle-wheel battleship premise to discuss science-fiction naval design, logistics, procurement, and environmental-war constraints.
segments.body: The discussion captures the stream's organizing subject before the wider audience Q&A takes over. Dr. Clarke frames the book as a science-fiction naval-history, procurement, and logistics discussion, then uses the paddle-wheel icebreaker battleship design to weigh maneuvering paddles, ice-breaking tradeoffs, repair access, eight-inch guns, command culture, and strategy.

evidence.note: The opening frames the stream as science-fiction naval history with procurement and logistics value centered on Glenn's book.
The discussion weighs the paddle arrangement, ice-breaking tradeoffs, repairability, and eight-inch paddle battleship concept.
The book discussion turns from the fictional premise to strategy, control, environmental-war pressures, and historical procurement analogies.
